About the role 

12-month contract 

Join QIC at a pivotal time as we implement a new enterprise technology GRC platform. As a Senior Business Systems Analyst, you’ll sit at the centre of business and technology, shaping solutions that strengthen governance, risk and compliance across the organisation. 

This is a hands-on role with real impact. You’ll work across major projects, BAU and targeted initiatives, owning business analysis end to end across the full software delivery lifecycle, from discovery and design through to build, testing and go live. Along the way, you’ll identify process improvement opportunities, solve complex business problems and ensure risk, controls and change impacts are embedded into every solution. 

Key responsibilities: 

Partner with business and technology teams to deliver the GRC platform and broader technology initiatives. 

Lead business analysis across the full software development lifecycle, including planning, design, build, testing and deployment. 

Translate business needs into clear requirements, user stories and process designs. 

Build strong stakeholder relationships and ensure alignment across business, technology and control teams. 

Identify process improvement opportunities and assess change and risk impacts. 

Support build, ST/SIT, UAT and implementation activities, including defect triage and end user readiness. 

Contribute to best practices, standards and continuous improvement across systems and services. 

About you 

You’re an experienced Business Systems Analyst with strong technical capability and a practical, delivery focused mindset. You’re comfortable working across business and technology teams and translating complex requirements into clear, actionable solutions. You’ll bring: 

Tertiary qualifications in Business/Finance, Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Management, or equivalent relevant experience.     

Hands-on experience with GRC platforms, including integrations, data migration, and control/reporting requirements. 

Experience leading, gathering and documenting business requirements, leading fit-gap analysis, as-is and to-be business process designs, functional configuration, testing, and end user training.  

Proven experience across the full software delivery lifecycle. 

Fundamental understanding of project and program lifecycle/methodologies (Waterfall, SDLC, Agile). 

Demonstrated proficiency in Azure DevOps (ADO) or similar and delivery tools to support effective backlog, requirement, and workflow management.

Job Title: AO8 Policy Manager and Research Analyst
Ad Text:
About the Role: AO8 Policy Manager and Research Analyst

Location: Brisbane, QLD

Start Date: ASAP

Contract Length: Until 30th June + possibility of extension

Pay: $123.91 p/h + super

Engagement Type: Contract

Focus: Strategic redesign of DFV, violent, and sexual offending programs

About the role:

Lead the evolution of evidence-informed rehabilitation within Queensland's custodial settings. This role requires a sophisticated blend of academic rigor and operational pragmatism to bridge global best practices with the mission of building safer communities. You will drive systemic reform by ensuring all rehabilitative services strictly adhere to the Risk-Need-Responsivity (RNR) model while maintaining an unwavering commitment to victim safety and perpetrator accountability.

Key Responsibilities:

RNR Integration: Ensure all program designs strictly match intervention intensity to offender risk levels and address dynamic criminogenic needs.
Global Benchmarking: Conduct comprehensive national and international jurisdictional scans to map contemporary legislative and program frameworks.
Evidence Synthesis: Perform specialist literature reviews of DFV and offending research, focusing on trauma-informed methodologies and emerging trends.
Program Mapping & Audit: Conduct end-to-end audits of current custodial programs, assessing their operational viability, clinical risks, and financial impact.
Modular Design: Architect a "Base + Module" program framework that allows for tailored interventions based on specific offender profiles.
Technical Scoping: Produce detailed blueprints for new models, including implementation costs, resource specifications, and delivery protocols.
Stakeholder Leadership: Manage expert input from SMEs and design engagement strategies to build consensus among competing interests.

Key Capabilities:

Deep understanding of criminology or social policy, specifically regarding the RNR framework and the complexities of domestic, family, and sexual violence.
Ability to translate specialist literature and comparative analyses into a seamless, integrated program roadmap.
Strong research background
Proven ability to coordinate with Subject Matter Experts and translate technical insights into actionable project requirements.
Expertise in producing high-level consolidated appraisals and comparative reports for executive review.
Capacity to foster a collaborative culture and respond positively to shifting project priorities.

Qualifications:

Criminology degree
Legal/law degree or equivalent
Extensive Research
RNR framework

Other Requirements:

Operational Flexibility: The incumbent may be required to work outside of normal hours and undertake travel as necessary.
Role Scope: Flexibility to undertake other duties as required outside of primary accountabilities to meet project goals

Eligibility:

Candidates must be a subject matter expert in criminology or social policy with a deep understanding of the RNR framework and the complexities of domestic, family, sexual offending and violent offending.
Also are motivated by the opportunity to apply your research expertise to real-world systemic reform in a high-stakes environment.

Benefits of Contracting with Randstad:

Access to benefits platforms with discounts at major retailers
weekly pay runs
Exclusive access to roles before they go to market
Employee assistance programme, including counselling services

If you would like to be considered for these roles, this is a great opportunity to tell Randstad about you. Please click "Apply Now" to submit your current resume.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.

We are dedicated to building a high‑performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.




Your New Role

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Operations Change, the Senior Associate, Operations Optimisation plays a critical role in leading and managing the end‑to‑end delivery of operational change and optimisation initiatives across the member service model and operational work chains.

This role blends project management discipline, delivery coordination, business analysis and change management, partnering closely with cross‑functional teams and vendors to translate requirements into executable plans, manage delivery milestones, and ensure initiatives are implemented safely and embedded into BAU operations.

The Senior Associate directly supports our Member Operations team by adapting operational processes to enable efficiency gains, risk reduction, product and service model changes, industry developments and regulatory obligations. Success in this role will require a strong understanding of the service model, combined with the ability to plan, govern and deliver initiatives in complex, regulated operational environments to achieve the best outcomes for members.




In this role, you'll:


Lead the end‑to‑end delivery of operational initiatives using structured project management approaches, including discovery, scoping, planning, execution, implementation and benefits realisation.
Develop and maintain delivery plans, milestones, dependencies, RAID logs and governance artefacts, ensuring clarity of scope, accountability and timelines.
Coordinate delivery across Member Operations, Technology, Risk, Member Domain, Legal and external vendors, managing interdependencies and sequencing to protect BAU outcomes.
Lead end‑to‑end reviews of operational work chains, identifying inefficiencies, delivery risks and control gaps, and translating findings into prioritised initiatives and delivery plans.
Facilitate requirements gathering, impact assessment and design workshops, ensuring business needs are clearly defined and understood prior to delivery.
Support the evolution of the service model by defining and implementing improved ways of working, hand‑offs and operating rhythms between teams. Drive integration and cutover planning when onboarding new functions, systems or services, ensuring operational readiness and smooth transition into BAU.
Provide clear, concise and regular project status reporting, including progress against milestones, impacts, risks, issues and constraints.
Contribute qualitative and quantitative analysis to prioritisation and sequencing decisions, balancing delivery capacity, regulatory timelines and member impact.
Apply robust change and implementation management practices, ensuring stakeholder readiness, adoption, training and documentation of new processes and controls.
Embed strong risk and control thinking within all initiatives, ensuring processes, policies and systems meet compliance and governance expectations and promoting a culture of risk awareness and continuous improvement.

What You'll Need

Proven experience in project management, delivery management, business optimisation or operational change roles.
Demonstrated experience planning and delivering initiatives end‑to‑end in complex operational or regulated environments.
Strong understanding of project delivery fundamentals, including scope management, scheduling, dependency management, risk and issue management and benefits tracking.
Solid business analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process mapping, impact analysis and solution design.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with experience using tools such as Excel, Power BI, JIRA, Confluence and process mapping software (e.g. Visio).
Experience applying structured methodologies such as Lean, Six Sigma, Prosci, ADKAR, Prince2 or Agile/Hybrid delivery approaches (certification desirable).
Experience within financial services, superannuation, member‑based or highly regulated industries is highly regarded.
Excellent communication, facilitation and stakeholder management skills, with confidence engaging senior and cross‑functional audiences.
Ability to work autonomously while coordinating delivery across multiple teams in fast‑paced operational environments.
Working knowledge of systems used in Member Operations (e.g. CRM, KMS Lighthouse, Nintex) is desirable.

Life at AustralianSuper


AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.




What's Next

Apply now if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes, and would like the opportunity to work in a growing and rapidly evolving team delivering meaningful AI outcomes for our members.




Important Information

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy:



https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

About JANA


We are here for one reason: People. Together with our clients, we can change the lives of millions of Australians for the better.
JANA is a leading investment consulting firm with offices in Melbourne & Sydney. We provide investment advisory consulting to over 85 institutional clients, including superannuation funds, charities, insurers, foundations, and endowment funds. Our services include asset allocation, manager research and selection, and investment strategy advice. We foster close relationships with each client to understand their needs and identify unique investment opportunities for their benefit.


Your new role


The focus of this role is to work closely with the Quant and Risk Function to support the wider advisory business. This opportunity is best suited to an analytically oriented and motivated candidate with strong technical skills to help develop and support the quantitative tools and models that JANA uses to undertake investment research, formulate investment advice, and provide analysis and reporting. 


This role can be based in either our Sydney or Melbourne office and reports to the Head of Quant and Risk.


Some of your responsibilities will include:
Develop, maintain, and enhance quantitative models and tools to support investment research, portfolio construction, and risk analysis
Analyse portfolio performance, attribution, and risk across multi‑asset portfolios, including equities, fixed income, cash, and derivatives.
Build and manage data pipelines and database structures using internal and external data sources (e.g. market and vendor data)
Apply statistical techniques to large and complex datasets to generate insights, reports and dashboards for internal and external stakeholders
Create and maintain programmatic models to deliver systematic and repeatable analysis
Support the development of analytics and frameworks for performance attribution, risk monitoring and portfolio evaluation
Review and improve existing Excel and code-based models across the business
Contribute to the development of capital market assumptions and asset allocation analytics
About you 


Equally challenging and rewarding, this opportunity is perfect for someone with:
Strong quantitative, analytical, and problem‑solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail
Advanced capability in Excel, Python (or similar), and SQL, with an interest in using modern tools (including AI‑assisted workflows) to improve efficiency
Experience with data visualisation or business intelligence tools (e.g. Power BI, Dash) is desirable
Comfortable working with large, complex datasets and translating analysis into clear insights
A collaborative team player who enjoys project‑based work and working with diverse stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non‑technical audiences
A proactive, self‑motivated learner who enjoys developing new skills and improving existing systems and processes
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline preferred (flexibility for the right candidate); exposure to financial markets or superannuation is advantageous but not essential
Being part of the team at JANA is just the beginning

About the Position

The Manager, Quality and Performance supports the University’s compliance, enterprise risk, and quality assurance functions by assisting in the development, implementation, and maintenance of effective frameworks, policies, and processes. This work helps ensure the University meets its legislative and regulatory obligations, upholds good governance practices, and enhances organisational performance.




The Manager provides oversight of enterprise risk-related activities and supports areas across the University in identifying, monitoring, and managing key risks. The role also contributes to the preparation of reports, reviews, and communications for a range of internal and external stakeholders. Effective communication, collaboration, and client‑service skills are essential, along with the ability to work effectively across a complex organisation to promote consistent, high‑quality governance and compliance outcome.

 

About the Person

The Manager, Quality and Performance is a key role within the University, supporting the delivery of robust governance, enterprise risk, compliance, and quality assurance practices that strengthen institutional integrity and performance.




The ideal applicant will have:

·         Postgraduate qualification (or equivalent senior experience) in governance, law, business, public administration, higher education, or a related discipline.

·         Demonstrated experience leading governance, compliance, risk management, and quality assurance frameworks within a complex organisation, ideally in the higher education sector.

·         Strong capability in interpreting and applying legislative, regulatory, and external reporting requirements to support institutional compliance and performance.

·         Proven ability to develop clear, fit‑for‑purpose policies, reports, and advice, and to communicate effectively with executive leaders, governing bodies, and diverse stakeholders.

·         Highly collaborative, service‑oriented professional with excellent interpersonal skills and a sound understanding of ethical governance, EEO, and WHS principles.

 

This role requires a collaborative and service-oriented professional who can build effective relationships across the University and provide trusted advice to support sound governance and continuous improvement.

In this unique role you will sit at the intersection of pricing, product and performance for an innovative food retail business.

You’ll provide data that informs commercial decisions – providing insights on product categories and their performance, and measuring the success of campaigns in driving growth.

Working closely with marketing and cross-functional leaders, you’ll provide the insights that guide category strategy, campaign management and pricing across a large national retail network.

This is a hands-on, high-impact analyst role for someone who enjoys moving between deep analysis and presenting insights that support business decision-making.


What you’ll do

Deliver category, pricing and campaign analytics that inform commercial decisions
Creating weekly campaign reporting and post campaign analysis to inform business decision-making on future initiatives
Build forecast models to support targets, pricing matrices and range optimisation
Analyse product performance, customer trends and campaign outcomes, identifying opportunities and providing insights
Track and evaluate pricing strategy and competitor activity
Develop dashboards and reporting that drive action, not just visibility
Partner with marketing, operations and supply chain to influence outcomes.

About you

Proven experience in a commercial, pricing or category analytics role
Strong retail or FMCG background
Advanced Excel and BI capability (Power BI; SQL essential)
Confident communicator who can simplify complex data and happy presenting to business leaders
A commercially curious mindset with strong attention to detail
Ability to work autonomously and bring actionable insights to the table.

Why this role

Genuine ownership of pricing and category insights
Exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic decision-making
The chance to influence business decisions - not just report on them
Competitive salary and career development opportunities in a much-loved retail brand.

Apply online to take the next step in your career!

About Ticketek Entertainment Group​

Ticketek Entertainment Group is a global fan experience company that tickets, promotes and delivers live experiences that are impossible to forget.  In a distracted world where nothing beats real human moments, We make life better live!

Our Group includes; our Fan Experience Platform (Ticketek) that sells tickets and provides value added services, Event promoting, with businesses across Touring (TEG Touring), Sport (TEG Sport), and Family Experiences (TEG Experiences) and our digital business (Ovation) which focuses on delivering seamless data-driven outcomes for our fans and partners.

About the Role:

As Head of Analytics Transformation, you will define and execute TEG’s advanced analytics strategy and roadmap. You will partner with senior leadership to identify high-impact use cases and oversee the end-to-end delivery of data products. This pivotal role drives our multi-phase transformation, requiring a blend of strategic vision and expert stakeholder management.



Requirements



What does a day in the life look like?

Strategic Partnership: Partner with senior executives and stakeholders to define high-impact analytics initiatives aligned to commercial objectives. Translate strategic priorities into measurable use cases with clear ROI and value realization pathways.
Enterprise Analytics Leadership: Own the end-to-end analytics ecosystem—including BI, forecasting, and optimization—ensuring solutions are scalable, governed, and embedded into operational workflows.
Portfolio & Delivery Oversight: Lead a portfolio of analytics initiatives, establishing prioritization frameworks, delivery roadmaps, and governance structures. Ensure disciplined execution, adoption, and measurable business impact.
Data Product Ownership: Drive the development of reusable analytics assets and decision-support tools that shift analytics from insight generation to operational enablement.
Stakeholder Counselling: Form deep counselling relationships with stakeholders across all ranks to influence the buy-in and practical application of advanced analytics.
Executive Influence & Story Telling: Provide trusted advisory to leadership, translating complex analytics into compelling commercial narratives that articulate problem framing, quantified impact, and recommended actions.

About you

Essential experience and skills

10+ years of experience in analytics consulting or delivery roles, with a proven track record of commercially successful, project-led outcomes.
Extensive experience in analytics, data science, or consulting within consumer0facing or large enterprise environments such as media, entertainment, or retail.
Possess deep expertise across CDPs, CRM, marketing automation, and paid media ecosystems, including platforms like Salesforce, Tealium, Braze, and Google Marketing Platform.
Strong command of enterprise BI and semantic modelling platforms, such as Tableau, Microsoft Power BI, or Looker, to drive decision intelligence.
Strong project management experience with a history of delivering high-profile analytics products that achieve measurable commercial outcomes.
Exceptional communication skills with the ability to articulate complex problems concisely through high-quality narratives and executive-ready slide packs.
A passion for leading cross-functional teams and building inclusive, pragmatics cultures rooted in strong internal and external partnerships.
Broad knowledge across data domains, including AI and Machine Learning, paired with a relevant tertiary qualification; a MBA or postgraduate degree is highly regarded.


Benefits



Here’s a taste of what TEG offers:

Complimentary event tickets
Birthday and volunteering leave
Wellbeing discounts & flu vaccinations
Paid parental leave & free employee support (EAP)
Global rewards and recognition
Learning, development & career pathways
A diverse, inclusive, and passionate team

Equal opportunities

TEG is an equal opportunity employer committed to embrace diversity, respect, and care for our people and communities.

Are you a passionate and ambitious Senior Data Engineer ready to dive into an environment that fosters innovation, continuous learning, and professional growth? We're seeking talented individuals who are eager to tackle complex problems, build scalable solutions, and collaborate with some of the finest engineers in the entertainment industry.

Complex Projects, Creative Solutions: Dive into intricate projects that challenge and push boundaries. Solve complex technical puzzles and craft scalable solutions.
Accelerate Your Growth: Access mentorship, training, and hands-on experiences to level up your skills. Learn from industry experts and gain expertise in scaling software.

About the team
You'll be joining the Data Engineering team on an exciting mission to build a top-tier data platform that powers everything we do. We manage data from our in-house products like Stake and Kick, as well as third-party tools and services, centralizing it and ensuring it's reliable, scalable, and ready to drive smarter decisions across the business.

We're focused on advanced data tools and services that truly make a difference. Our goal is to help teams unlock the full potential of data, empowering them to create impactful outcomes for our customers and the business. If you're someone who loves tackling big challenges and shaping the future of data, you'll fit right in!

Key Responsibilities

Architect, design, and optimise scalable pipelines, orchestrating complex workflows and enabling efficient data processing from multiple sources.
Lead the development of secure, scalable, and cost-efficient data infrastructure using AWS services and Terraform, ensuring engineering best practices in infrastructure automation.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define and drive strategic data initiatives, including data lakehouse architecture, real-time data processing, and analytics enablement.
Own and enhance observability practices, implementing robust monitoring, alerting, and logging systems to minimise downtime and optimise performance.
Develop and execute automated data governance processes to ensure compliance with data lineage, classification, access control, GDPR, and other regulatory standards. This includes masking, encryption, and policy enforcement to protect sensitive data, especially in regulated markets.
Architect and optimise data pipelines for Change Data Capture (CDC), reconciliation workflows, and reliability enhancements.
Drive improvements in engineering best practices, including code quality, system design, documentation, and operational efficiency, to foster team efficiency and high-quality outcomes with measurable business value.
Identify opportunities and implement innovative solutions to enhance data platform capabilities, proactively solving performance bottlenecks and improving data reliability.
Perform advanced code reviews, mentor engineers and foster a collaborative, high-performance and collaborative engineering culture.

Qualifications

A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field or equivalent practical experience.
8+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role, with demonstrated expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT design, database management, and real-time data pipelines.
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python, or PySpark, with deep experience in building data pipelines leveraging AWS services such as Glue, Redshift, Kinesis, Lambda, S3, and DMS.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Apache Airflow), version control systems (e.g., GitHub), and big data technologies such as Spark or Hadoop.
Proven expertise in data governance, security, and compliance standards, particularly in regulated environments, including managing lineage, classification, access control, and PII.
Experience designing and implementing modern cloud-based data platforms, preferably on AWS, using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform.
Lead R&D efforts, exploring emerging technologies and evaluating their applications for system enhancement.
Knowledge of machine learning concepts and their integration with data platforms.
Strong problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills for engaging with cross-functional teams and addressing complex technical challenges.
Experience in technical leadership skills, including mentoring teams, providing strategic guidance, and shaping organisational strategies.
Some of the perks of joining us:
Championing Engineering Excellence to influence data driven impact across global scale software products.
Work alongside the top 5% of engineering talent in Australia using a vast AWS cloud native and big data technology stack.
Exposure to building global, large-scale volume data pipelines, data warehouses, and datalakes which are consuming requests at thousands per second frequency.
Access to over 9,000 courses across our Learning and Development Platform.
EAP access for you and your family.
Be rewarded with lucrative annual bonuses.
Give back with a paid volunteer day.
Fuel your day with daily breakfast and open pantries brimming with unlimited snacks and refreshments, all on the house.
Break up the week with on site remedial massage Wednesdays.
In house full-time barista's providing you your daily coffee needs.
Weekly team lunches and happy hour in the office from 4pm on Fridays.
Enjoy a bustling office with the option for up to 2 days work from home per week.
Fun office environment with F1 simulators, table tennis and all your favourite gaming consoles.

- Location: Melbourne / Hybrid working

Daily-Rate: Up to $1,400/day + Super

Talenza is partnering with a leading enterprise organisation to secure an experienced Analytics Specialist to join a high-performing Data & Analytics function. This is an exciting opportunity to apply advanced analytics and machine learning expertise to solve complex business problems and drive impactful, data-led decisions.

About the Role

As an Analytics Specialist, you will work closely with business stakeholders and analytics leaders to design, develop and deliver high-value analytical solutions. You'll play a key role in translating business challenges into data-driven insights, leveraging modern tools and techniques across large, complex datasets.

Key Responsibilities

Partner with business stakeholders to develop and refine analytical use cases

Translate business problems into analytical and machine learning solutions

Analyse, interpret and model large datasets to generate actionable insights

Rapidly test and iterate hypotheses to solve complex challenges

Develop and present clear data visualisations and insights to non-technical audiences

Provide subject matter expertise in machine learning to internal teams

Lead and coordinate analytics and data science initiatives across onshore and offshore teams

Contribute to agile delivery, including sprint planning and effort estimation

Ensure compliance with internal policies and relevant regulatory standards

Share knowledge and promote best practices across the analytics community

About You

You are a technically strong and commercially minded analytics professional who thrives in fast-paced environments and enjoys solving real-world problems through data.

Key Skills & Experience:

Experience working with large and complex datasets to generate insights

Strong skills in Python, SQL & Databricks

Experience working in collaborative development environments

Comfortable working in Linux-based systems

Strong communication skills with the ability to bridge business and technical teams

Exposure to machine learning techniques and frameworks

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in a relevant field, or equivalent practical experience

Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative discipline (preferred)

Why Apply?

High-impact role within a mature data and analytics environment

Long-term contract through to end of 2026

Opportunity to work on diverse, business-critical initiatives

Competitive daily rate

About the role

Zezamii is building Zezamii Vision, an AI-powered video analytics platform for real-time event detection on live camera feeds. The business is now productising the solution into a scalable platform that combines live inference, event management, client administration, and reusable machine learning pipelines. This role leads the ML side of the product across the full lifecycle, from model fine-tuning and evaluation through to deployment support and reusable training pipelines.

Initial focus

Fine-tune and improve production computer vision models for live event detection.

Increase accuracy across edge cases, difficult scenes, and extreme scenarios.

Help create a reusable Azure ML training architecture that can support future use cases and industries.

Key responsibilities

Fine-tune and improve computer vision models for real-time event detection.

Build and maintain reusable training and retraining workflows in Azure ML.

Design robust evaluation processes, metrics, and error analysis pipelines.

Work with labelled datasets, annotation feedback loops, and data quality improvement.

Support deployment of models into production inference environments.

Collaborate with platform engineers building the live streaming, event tracking, and dashboard components.

Help define the reusable ML architecture for future use cases and industries.

Contribute to model monitoring, versioning, and continuous improvement practices.

Required skills and experience

Strong experience in Python for machine learning and data workflows.

Hands-on experience with computer vision model development and fine-tuning.

Minimum 2-3 years of experience in computer vision model development.

Experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow, preferably PyTorch.

Experience with object detection, activity recognition, video analytics, or event detection.

Experience with model training, validation, experiment tracking, and performance optimisation.

Experience deploying or supporting ML models in production environments.

Minimum 1-2 years of experience in production ML model deployment.

Experience with cloud ML platforms such as Azure ML, AWS SageMaker, or GCP Vertex AI.

Strong understanding of dataset quality, false positive/false negative trade-offs, and edge-case analysis.

Preferred experience

Azure ML Studio and Azure-based ML workflows.

Live video streams, CCTV, RTSP, or real-time inference systems.

Docker, containers, and cloud deployment pipelines.

Safety-critical or compliance-sensitive environments.

Designing reusable ML systems rather than one-off models.

What success looks like

Production models achieve improved production-grade accuracy.

The training pipeline becomes reusable across future Zezamii Vision use cases.

Model deployment becomes more repeatable and reliable.

ML workflows are well-documented and scalable.

Job Title: Global Paid Media and Growth Lead
Ad Text:
Global Paid Media and Growth Lead
Set, own and deliver the paid growth strategy
Lead a multi-million dollar paid media function
Circa $190k - $220k plus super and bonus
Join a global, high-growth digital platform operating at the forefront of the creative, content and AI ecosystem.

Ownership of a global, multi-million dollar paid media function


Direct exposure to executive leadership and CEO


Strong in-house creative and analytics capability (you won’t be flying blind)


Real focus on experimentation, innovation and AI-driven growth


Opportunity to shape the future of a high-impact acquisition channel


If you’re looking for a role where you can operate at scale, influence strategy and push the boundaries of paid growth, this is a standout opportunity.  Apply online or call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset - 0413 463 043.

Grant Thornton Australia is one of the world's leading independently-owned and managed accounting, advisory and consulting firms.

Our culture is underpinned by a commitment to our clients, people and communities, and our promise to ‘Reach for Remarkable’ by providing proven expertise, principled execution, and personalised experience.

In Australia, we’re proud to support the careers of over 1,500 remarkable people. With six offices nationally and partner firms in more than 145 markets, wherever you want to go, we can take you there.

About this role

We are looking for a Senior Manager to join our Risk Consulting team who will work closely with our clients to deliver practical technology risk solutions including areas such SOC-2 reporting (security, availability and integrity), cyber security, data risk / data governance, open banking accreditation, privacy and IT audit. This will be offered initially as a 6-month contract.

Key responsibilities

Lead and deliver a range of technology risk projects including SOC-2, cyber security, data risk / data governance, GS 007, open banking accreditation, privacy and IT audit.
Execute IT risk reviews against security frameworks such as COBIT, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001 and NIST
Client relationship management including planning, budget preparation and tracking, scope determination and value add
Coaching and developing a team of seniors/consultants/associates
Actively managing risk matters
Clearly articulating the risks and opportunities associated with IT to a broad range of technical and executive stakeholders
Ability to communicate and demonstrate a logical risk based approach to the review of technology
Providing clients with pragmatic recommendations to address areas of concern, risk or opportunity

Skills & experience

We’re looking for an experienced leader skilled in supporting and motivating their team through training and mentoring and fosters a culture of collaboration and inclusion. This role will suit a results driven, team orientated professional with the following skills, experience and attributes:

IT risk and security experience management experience in a private/public practice environment
Experience with auditing standards and industry frameworks such as SOC-2, GS 007, IIA/ISACA, open banking, COBIT, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001 and/or NIST
A passion for providing exceptional service and delivery to clients 
CISA, CRISC, CISM, CISSP or CA / CPA qualifications, or steps towards achieving these qualifications, will be highly regarded as will experience in a professional services environment
Ability to articulate your understanding of major IT systems and their deployment across organisations
Ability to work independently with a strong sense of ownership

Reach your remarkable

At Grant Thornton we do things differently because we understand that when you strive for better and care about what you do remarkable things are possible. We’re a dynamic and authentic mix of backgrounds, perspectives and ways of thinking. We’re driven by our shared purpose – we care for our people, clients and communities and support them to thrive.

With us, you’ll be exposed to challenging and rewarding opportunities – building your confidence and capabilities at every step. You’ll be supported, motivated and inspired by a team of passionate and caring collaborators and leaders. You’ll be empowered to build a career path that’s tailored to you and encouraged to make a meaningful difference. And we’ll make sure the excellence you deliver and the impact you make is always seen, felt and celebrated.

Remarkable perks that put you first:

Flexible working options to support you achieve a more balanced approach to life,
Free annual gym membership to help keep you flexible, fit, and healthy
Active employee networks and social committees to get you involved in the things you are passionate about
Family focused support to suit your parental and/or caregiving needs, including 26 weeks parental leave and a parental leave superannuation scheme
Mental health and wellbeing resources and coaching so you look after yourself both inside and out.
And many more!

About this role 

We are seeking a Director – Technology Risk who is eager to help shape and grow our expanding practice. This is an opportunity for a senior professional with strong technical foundations and industry experience to step into a leadership role, influence our strategy and make a tangible impact in our practice. You will work closely with the Sydney Partner to shape our approach, enable our team and deliver solutions that help clients navigate complex technology risks in an increasingly digital world. 

Key responsibilities 

Drive growth by expanding key accounts and originating new opportunities in our target industries of financial services, government, critical infrastructure and higher education 

Shape our response to market developments including legislation, regulatory changes, litigation trends and emerging technologies such as AI, influencing service design and go-to-market strategy. 

Build market presence by representing the firm at internal and external events, and engaging with industry and professional groups such as ASFA, ISACA and COBA 

Enhance our technology risk services across cyber security, data governance, regulatory compliance, technology and operational resilience, and IT audit 

Build capability and capacity within the team by mentoring our managers and junior resources 

Oversee larger, multi-stakeholder engagements with accountability for scope, budget and quality — not just delivery but ensuring strategic value is delivered to clients 

Own co-source internal audit relationships through effective relationship management 

Partner with boards and executives to provide pragmatic, risk-based insights that strengthen resilience and enable confident decision-making 

Skills & experience  

At least 10 years of experience in technology risk and assurance, with strong technical foundations and the ambition to help shape and grow a practice. 

Deep expertise in technology risk, including cyber maturity frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST-CSF, NIST SP series, COBIT, PCI-DSS), regulatory compliance and IT audit 

Recognised industry presence demonstrated through speaking engagements, thought leadership or active participation in professional forums and events 

Industry specialism in financial services, government, critical infrastructure and/or higher education, with an understanding of sector-specific risks and regulatory environments (eg CPS 234, CPS 230 and SOCI) 

Strong understanding of audit requirements and methodologies, with experience in SOC 2, GS 007, ASAE 3402 and SOX engagements 

Demonstrated ability and ambition to contribute to client and market growth, with experience managing key accounts and identifying new opportunities. 

Demonstrated ability to mentor and develop junior resources and managers to build team capability and capacity

Deloitte Services Pty Ltd

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

What You'll Be Working On

Customer segmentation projects using clustering techniques

Enabling broader business use cases through incremental data science projects

Supporting the evolution of the organisation's analytics and machine learning capability

Skills & Experience

Essential:

Strong experience with Python & SQL

Experience working with large and complex datasets

Strong communication skills-ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences

Experience working in collaborative, cross-functional environments

Desirable:

Familiarity with BI/reporting tools and data visualisation

Experience in customer analytics, marketing analytics, or digital analytics

Understanding of clustering, segmentation, and other unsupervised learning techniques

Exposure to agile delivery environments

About Brighter Super:
At Brighter Super it’s all about the personal connections, guiding our members to retire confidently as a member first fund – we live by the philosophy that our members come first in everything we do. Since 1965, we have been a trusted and reliable provider of investment, advice and insurance services. We’re a 100% member-owned, Queensland-based, open superannuation fund, built on the foundation of three funds (LGIASuper, Energy Super and Suncorp Super) where we manage more than $37 billion in retirement savings for over 348,000 members. Our members come from our foundation industries of government, finance, electrical and energy sectors, alongside a diverse cross-section of the wider community.

Our Brighter Super team is essential to our success, embodying our core values of Aspiring for our members, Unlock innovation, Performance with purpose, and Winning together. We value every Brighter team member – Brighter people, Brighter futures, Brighter together.  This commitment fosters a supportive and collaborative work environment where each individual is empowered to thrive. Together, we are building a brighter future.
  
About your new Role: 
Your role sits within Brighter Super’s Portfolio department and reports directly to the Head of Portfolio. This role is critical to delivering fund-wide programs and cross-functional projects that will support the delivery of our strategic plan. This role is reliant on strong key business stakeholder relationships to support the development and analysis of user-centric business requirements under the EPMO at Brighter Super.

Develop strong working relationships with business stakeholders at all levels across all Business Units and Support functions.
Identify gaps and improvements to our current business analysis methodologies ensuring alignment to our Governance and delivery management frameworks, practices and processes.
Document high-level requirements, user stories and detailed acceptance criteria.
Facilitate requirements workshops with internal and external teams, showcase and UAT sessions.
Conduct training sessions with stakeholders and end users and prepare training and supporting documentation.
Raise and support mitigation and resolution of risks and issues using established tools and frameworks.
About You: 
You bring proven experience as a confident and capable Business Analyst, with experience within the Financial Services sector. Your skill set allows you to analyse complex problems, translate business needs into clear requirements, and contribute effectively to strategic and operational improvements. You are an experienced and confident BA with experience within the Financial Services Sector:

Facilitated workshops and are very confident in your own presentation skills
Created workflows in BPMN Diagram Maker
Proven ability to successfully validate and document requirements for developers and testers in form of epics, features and user stories.
Extensive experience in working in Agile environments with a cross functional team
Ability to work autonomously in an environment with shirting priorities and timelines
Why choose Brighter Super:  
We pride ourselves on being a great place to work, where you matter!  Here are some things that you will be able to enjoy; 

Workplace flexibility, with the option to work up to 50% of your week from home
Up to 18 weeks paid parental leave + super  
Purchased leave

Employee Assistance Program
Holistic benefits and well-being offer through Perkbox

Job description:



● Unlock meaningful insights from client data

● Experience in working across functions

● Gain skills with Australia's largest provider of government-funded hearing care

● Macquarie Park Office with hybrid work arrangements

Want to improve the lives of Australians, no matter who they are?

Join us and build a career and future you'll be proud of.

It matters more at Hearing Australia

Hearing Australia is a trusted leader in our field. Working across the country, we exist to support and empower people through hearing health when and where they need it.

Your opportunity

As Customer Data Insight Analyst, you'll be reporting to the Head of Client Value Management and will be responsible for building predictive models to forecast client behaviour and assist in the development of client segmentation models to enable personalised marketing and value propositions for different client groups. Working side by side with people who go the extra mile, you'll also love the feeling of helping children and adults who need us. Here's how you'll do it:

Deliver Data-Driven Insights to Drive Client Engagement, Retention and Commercial Impact
Develop Client Segmentation and Predictive Models to Inform Strategy
Collaborate with IT and Finance Reporting Teams to Enhance Data Infrastructure
Leverage CRM Platforms and Stay Aligned with Evolving Data Technologies
Deliver Insightful Reporting to Drive Business Decisions
Communicate Insights Effectively and Embed Data-Driven Thinking Across the Business

What You'll Bring

We believe the successful candidate will have a proven track record in driving client retention and increasing lifetime value. You'll bring:

Proven experience in extracting, analysing, and interpreting complex behavioural and commercial data to generate strategic insights and business recommendations.
Hands-on experience in campaign reporting and performance analysis, with the ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights.
Strong capability in developing client segmentation frameworks and building predictive models.
Skilled in analytics tools such as SQL, Python, R, or similar, and data visualization platforms like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
Strong hands-on experience with CRM platforms, ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Customer Insights Journeys (CIJ), including customer data, campaign tracking, and attribution.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Marketing, Economics, or a related field.
Solid understanding of data quality, integration, and governance, including working with first- and third-party data sources.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver insights within tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

What matters most? It's simple - your drive to help anyone and everyone who needs us. And you'll see your role as a real responsibility and an incredible opportunity to make an impact on lives across the country.

Why Hearing Australia?

There's so much to love about a role with us. But our people say there's three things that set us and their careers apart. They're about:

● How we do what we do: Our vision is to deliver the best hearing solutions for anyone, anytime, anywhere. At Hearing Australia, you'll know you're making a contribution that really means something

● Who we work with: Here, you'll support and empower clients of all backgrounds. That's what drives our teams and what makes them so special

● Where we're going next: As our industry evolves, we're committed to learning and improving what we do and how we do it and building an organisation where everyone can thrive.

What does that mean for you?

As Customer Data Insights Analyst, you'll know that every day, your work is helping children and adults who need us. Just ask Sandeep, one of our IT professionals about why they choose to work with us:

"The work we do in helping people hear sound again and for the very first time is quite inspirational. Everything we do here is purpose-driven."

You'll also enjoy a range of benefits, including:

Attractive employee benefits (salary sacrifice superannuation, HCF private health cover discount, well-being programs, salary packaging, discounts across an exciting range of retailers)
Paid parental leave
Free, onsite staff parking
Employee recognition awards program
Flexible work arrangement opportunities to support work, personal and family commitments

Let's create the gold standard of hearing care

We will require the successful candidate to be willing to have a Police Check conducted (we pay), a current driver's licence and must have rights to work in Australia.

Power BI Consultant 

At Ascendunt, we help organisations turn raw data into meaningful, trusted insights that drive confident decision‑making. Our consultants sit at the intersection of business logic, data modelling, and visual storytelling—ensuring that every dashboard not only looks exceptional but reflects the true intent behind the metrics.

This role plays a critical part in transforming functional requirements into intuitive, high‑performance Power BI experiences that executives rely on to understand performance, identify trends, and make strategic decisions.

Role Purpose
The Power BI Consultant (“The Storyteller”) partners with Business Analysts, Technical Analysts, and business stakeholders to design and deliver reporting that is accurate, insightful, and visually compelling. This role goes beyond simply building dashboards—it uncovers the narrative behind the data, ensuring that every visual aligns with the underlying business rules, data model, and KPI definitions.

The role is central to translating complex datasets into clear, consumable insights that support operational, regulatory, and strategic decision‑making.

Ad Text:
The role:  

Reporting to the Program Director Better Hospital Flows project, you will be responsible for analysing, manipulating and presenting data.
The Queensland Government is investing in improved hospital services through real-time data and targeted technology upgrades under the Health Plan Commitment. A key priority action is Better Hospital Flows (GEC124), which focuses on modernising digital systems to improve hospital workflows, patient flow and visibility of bed availability across the state. Delivery of GEC124 is being progressed through two related initiatives:

Project 3a, which focuses on enhancements to the Statewide Management Information System (SWMIS) to improve the timeliness, quality and consistency of core operational data; and
Project 3b, which focuses on the assessment, planning and implementation of a statewide Patient Flow Insights capability that translates operational data into actionable insights.

Government Election Commitment (GEC) 124 – Better Hospital Flows – Project 3b comprises two phases: Phase 1 - Assessment and Phase 2 - Implementation.
This role relates to Project 3b: Assessment and Implementation and hereafter refers to it as the “project”.

In addition to this, you will be responsible for:

Plan and design analyses, extract, manipulate and perform statistical analysis of data using statistical software and prepare reports to disseminate information to meet the needs of the organisation.
Query missing, incomplete or inconsistent data using analysis software, including but not limited to Oracle SQL, SAS, R or Excel and undertake analysis and write queries and reports as required.
Display a wide variety of visual reports, KPI scorecards and dashboards, utilizing programs such as Power BI and Tableau.
Inspect, cleanse, transform and analyse raw data to discover useful information, inform conclusions and support effective decision making.
As required, design and build scalable data models and enabling advanced analytic capabilities that provide meaningful business value through easy-to-comprehend data visualisations.
Collaborate with key stakeholders to deliver relevant business insights based on identified business requirements.
Work independently through unstructured tasks.
Prepare confidential, complex reports, briefings and correspondence and initiate action associated with financial, procurement, human resource and other matters as required by the Program Director, Better Hospital Flows.

Are you right for this role?

To be successful in the role, you will have demonstrated capacity to analyse complex system issues and processes, provide specification/solution development and design and business case development. (NFL: Supports Strategic Direction).

Furthermore, you will have demonstrated:

Demonstrated high level of knowledge and experience in statistical methods and concepts and demonstrated ability to appropriately use statistical procedures and interpret statistical information.
Demonstrated high level of programming, data manipulation and analysis ability, including experience using statistical programming packages to interrogate, extract, and manipulate data, conduct descriptive and inferential statistical analysis and produce figures and tables; and experience with large and complex data sets and relational databases.
Demonstrated high level of interpersonal skills and written communication skills, including ability to consult with personnel at all levels in the organisation, external organisations and professional groups, ability to communicate technical information to a non-technical audience, and report writing skills.
Demonstrated ability to prioritise tasks and to effectively monitor and co-ordinate stakeholders to achieve outcomes and to meet deadlines.
Demonstrated ability to manage and develop members of a professional team in a working environment supporting quality human resource management practices.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading

Diverse work culture
Career training and development

Full time position based in either Sydney or Melbourne




We are Woolworths Group

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 




If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




What you’ll do

Reporting to the Store Loss Insights Manager, the Fraud Insights Lead is a pivotal role within the Store Loss team, primarily responsible for leveraging data and technology to identify and mitigate internal and external fraud. To support the business in identifying coaching opportunities and internal/external dishonesty by utilizing advanced technology and data systems. This role packages actionable insights for investigation and store management to drive performance conversations and profit protection.

Identify New Fraud Behaviors: Proactively research and identify emerging fraud patterns and behaviors within the store environment.

AI Implementation: Leverage AI-driven methodologies to automate the detection of suspicious activities and improve the accuracy of fraud alerts.

Data Engineering: Work within the GCP (Google Cloud Platform) to develop and manage new datasets and create sophisticated rules for real-time fraud monitoring.

System Validation: Use multiple systems to validate fraudulent behaviors and quantify their impact on profit, packaging these findings for formal investigations like CCTV, POS,R10 etc.

Collaboration: Drive the overarching fraud prevention strategy by collaborating with internal and external partners to identify and fix systemic loopholes.

Stakeholder Engagement: Partner across Store Support, State Operations, and Project teams to embed collaborative initiatives and incident reduction processes.

Loss Priorities: Contribute to the development of strategic Loss priorities and support the monitoring of controls to prevent incident recurrence

Coaching & Training: Upskill store managers to conduct effective investigations and lead team member performance conversations.

Customer First: Ensure all planning and prioritisation focus on the customer while keeping processes simple and unambiguous for store teams.




What you’ll bring

Deep understanding of internal and external fraud, retail processes and supplier mechanics

Experience creating rules and datasets within Google Cloud Platform

Practical experience applying AI technologies to solve business problems

Proficient in Scan Assist, Auror, March Networks (CCTV), and store front-end systems (1 POS, Store Central, Retalix, Scan & Go)

Proficiency in Google Suite, knowledge of Sheets, Docs and Drive

High-level negotiation and presentation skills, with the ability to gain stakeholder agreement on complex decisions




What you’ll experience

Our Team Members are at the heart of everything we do and we’re always looking for ways to support your career journey and reward great work:

Competitive salary package and bonus

Team discounts across our range of Woolworths Group brands you know and love and a robust rewards program that celebrates and incentivises purpose-driven work.

A global business with endless career possibilities around every corner and across every discipline – with valuable exposure to a vast and exciting business network.

A range of programs to help you prioritise and manage your wellbeing, including 24/7 access to the Sonder app.

A progressive and competitive leave policy that gives you more space for what matters to you




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.

Ad Text:
Important leadership role helping shape cash-related policy

Impactful work that makes a difference to the Australian people

Permanent opportunity, hybrid working

About the team

We are seeking an experienced economist or public-policy professional to join the Analysis and Policy (A&P) area of the RBA's Note Issue Department.

The Analysis and Policy section develops evidence-based policies to support the Bank's strategic priorities in relation to issuing secure and reliable banknotes, and shaping the future of money. We conduct research on cash use, access and acceptance to understand how the cash ecosystem is evolving and to identify potential policy measures to support cash as a viable means of payment. We also facilitate public-sector coordination on cash initiatives, working closely with Council of Financial Regulator (CFR) agencies and the ACCC.

A&P is leading a number of high-priority workstreams to address challenges in cash distribution.

About the role

As a manager in A&P, you will:


Provide economic and policy advice that supports priority topics and contributes to NI meeting its strategic goals, including the Bank's strategic priority to support access to physical cash.

Communicate effectively to relevant internal and external stakeholders in support of the Bank's functions and objectives.

Provide coaching and development opportunities for analysts, to support them in achieving their goals.

Contribute to the broader strategic direction and leadership of the Note Issue Department, sharing accountability with other leaders for overall performance of deliverables and outcomes.

Foster an open and inclusive culture to enable your team to deliver high quality and impactful work.

About you


Excellent critical thinking skills to guide and produce targeted analysis on complex policy issues.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Demonstrated people leadership skills and ability to nurture a culture of trust and collaboration in line with the Bank's open and dynamic ambition. This includes the ability to develop your team to deliver results and build skills over time.

An ability to write clearly and succinctly, with the purpose and intended audience in mind.

Strong work planning, prioritisation and organisation skills.

An ability and willingness build and maintain a culture of trust and collaboration.

Have a relevant tertiary qualification, such as in economics, finance, public policy or statistics.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Job Title: Senior Capital & Liquidity Lead - Financial Analytics
Ad Text:
About the role: 

As the Senior Capital & Liquidity Lead - Financial Analytics, you will be a key member of the Financial Analytics team within the Finance & Data Division. You will hold Line 1 accountability for capital, liquidity and balance sheet risk management activities and provide forward‑looking insights that support informed decision making across the Bank.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Lead the development and delivery of the Bank’s ICAAP and Recovery Plan, ensuring alignment with APRA prudential standards and regulatory expectations.

Design and deliver enterprise-wide stress testing frameworks, including scenario design, model development and interpretation of outcomes to inform capital planning and strategic decision making.

Present capital and liquidity insights to Executive Committees and the Board, supporting strategic balance sheet decisions.

Develop and maintain liquidity analysis and metrics, including Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) capability.

Ensuring strong governance, documentation and alignment across capital, liquidity, ALM, FTP and forecasting frameworks.

Providing technical leadership, coaching and knowledge sharing within the Financial Analytics team.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will bring strong technical capability, sound judgement and the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders.

You will also demonstrate:

Significant experience in financial institutions, with strong knowledge of balance sheet management, capital, liquidity and financial risk management.

Proven experience leading ICAAP, capital management, stress testing or balance sheet risk frameworks within a regulated financial institution.

The ability to design, interpret and explain complex financial models, stress testing outcomes and risk frameworks.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and the ability to translate technical analysis into clear insights for non‑technical audiences.

Well developed communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence working with Executives and senior leaders.

A proactive, risk‑aware mindset and commitment to continuous improvement and professional development.

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Business, Economics, Actuarial Studies or a related discipline is essential.

Postgraduate or professional qualifications (CA, CPA, CFA or similar) and relevant industry memberships are desirable.

A workplace for you to be you. 

Come and work with a team that values your unique perspective, contribution, career development and growth.

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contact centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.
